---
schema: verdi.decisionconflict/v1
covers: 1742395abe517bcc9bf38246f925eb78d4e076bc
findings:
  - { id: judged-dc3-remote-sealed-link, kind: judged, text: "story dc-3 emits one uniform link grammar for every design-branch entry (/b/\u003cbranch-escaped\u003e/board/spec/\u003cname\u003e) with no local/remote-only split, but parent feature dc-5 mandates that only a local design branch opens as an authoring wall (managed worktrees are cut from local branches only) while a remote-only branch renders sealed with its remoteness disclosed; if the per-branch board address is backed by a managed worktree, linking remote-only entries to it either violates the local-only worktree rule or produces a dead link, and no supersedes/exempts edge is declared against feature dc-5 for the link-grammar decision (dc-4 cites feature dc-5 only for the in-review chip) (confidence 0.55)", target_ref: spec/workbench-directory }
  - { id: judged-dc3-address-as-distinction, kind: judged, text: "story dc-3 differentiates entries by their address space — default-branch entries keep unprefixed addresses while design-branch entries get per-branch addresses — which keys a user-visible distinction off the entry's branch/address, in tension with parent feature dc-2's 'the status is the distinction, never the address'; story dc-2 declares conformance for grouping only, leaving dc-3's address-keyed link differentiation without a declared edge (confidence 0.30)", target_ref: spec/workbench-directory }
sweep_provenance: { adr_corpus_digest: sha256:37517e5f3dc66819f61f5a7bb8ace1921282415f10551d2defa5c3eb0985b570, decisions_scanned: [spec/directory-home#dc-1, spec/directory-home#dc-2, spec/directory-home#dc-3, spec/directory-home#dc-4, spec/directory-home#dc-5, spec/workbench-directory#dc-1, spec/workbench-directory#dc-2, spec/workbench-directory#dc-3, spec/workbench-directory#dc-4, spec/workbench-directory#dc-5] }
digest: sha256:d6789e42cf545f7c4f78c7debfaef4af1809c54cb08cd9d3a45e74893b7f5194
integrity: sha256:8e34ba4ae79fd9cceaabf13a91292effddb237fd578b6633de4dd66c61edd926
judge_integrity: { stdin_b64: , raw_result: "{\"findings\":[{\"id\":\"dc3-remote-sealed-link\",\"text\":\"story dc-3 emits one uniform link grammar for every design-branch entry (/b/\u003cbranch-escaped\u003e/board/spec/\u003cname\u003e) with no local/remote-only split, but parent feature dc-5 mandates that only a local design branch opens as an authoring wall (managed worktrees are cut from local branches only) while a remote-only branch renders sealed with its remoteness disclosed; if the per-branch board address is backed by a managed worktree, linking remote-only entries to it either violates the local-only worktree rule or produces a dead link, and no supersedes/exempts edge is declared against feature dc-5 for the link-grammar decision (dc-4 cites feature dc-5 only for the in-review chip)\",\"confidence\":0.55,\"target\":\"spec/workbench-directory\"},{\"id\":\"dc3-address-as-distinction\",\"text\":\"story dc-3 differentiates entries by their address space — default-branch entries keep unprefixed addresses while design-branch entries get per-branch addresses — which keys a user-visible distinction off the entry's branch/address, in tension with parent feature dc-2's 'the status is the distinction, never the address'; story dc-2 declares conformance for grouping only, leaving dc-3's address-keyed link differentiation without a declared edge\",\"confidence\":0.3,\"target\":\"spec/workbench-directory\"}]}" }

---
# Decision-conflict report

## Computed (declared-edge completeness)

(none)

## Judged (undeclared-conflict sweep)

- **judged-dc3-remote-sealed-link** [UNDISPOSITIONED]: story dc-3 emits one uniform link grammar for every design-branch entry (/b/<branch-escaped>/board/spec/<name>) with no local/remote-only split, but parent feature dc-5 mandates that only a local design branch opens as an authoring wall (managed worktrees are cut from local branches only) while a remote-only branch renders sealed with its remoteness disclosed; if the per-branch board address is backed by a managed worktree, linking remote-only entries to it either violates the local-only worktree rule or produces a dead link, and no supersedes/exempts edge is declared against feature dc-5 for the link-grammar decision (dc-4 cites feature dc-5 only for the in-review chip) (confidence 0.55)
- **judged-dc3-address-as-distinction** [UNDISPOSITIONED]: story dc-3 differentiates entries by their address space — default-branch entries keep unprefixed addresses while design-branch entries get per-branch addresses — which keys a user-visible distinction off the entry's branch/address, in tension with parent feature dc-2's 'the status is the distinction, never the address'; story dc-2 declares conformance for grouping only, leaving dc-3's address-keyed link differentiation without a declared edge (confidence 0.30)
